---
title: "How To Train Your Researcher (HTTYR)"
subtitle: "EROP Playbook"
authors: "EROP Team"
date: last-modified
---

## Introduction

This page is specific to the Early Research Opportunity Program (EROP) at SUTD.
It walks mentors and students through the **"I Do, We Do, You Do"** teaching
strategy — a scaffolded approach that progressively transfers responsibility from
mentor to student.

::: {.callout-note}
These guides are living documents. They are meant to reduce friction, not to be
treated as the definitive rulebook for doing research.
:::

---

## The "I Do, We Do, You Do" Framework

The framework breaks mentorship into three phases of decreasing mentor involvement
and increasing student autonomy.

### I Do — Modelling

The mentor demonstrates the skill or process while thinking aloud.
Students observe and ask questions.

**Example:**
> *The mentor reads an AI research paper aloud, narrating the decisions they make
> as they skim the abstract, identify the key contribution, and assess
> experimental validity.*

### We Do — Guided Practice

The mentor and student work through a task together.
The student takes the lead; the mentor provides real-time prompts and feedback.

**Example:**
> *The student reads a new paper while the mentor asks guiding questions:
> "What problem is this solving?", "What baseline did they compare against?",
> "Would this method work in a different domain?"*

### You Do — Independent Practice

The student completes the task independently and presents their work.
The mentor reviews and gives structured feedback afterward.

**Example:**
> *The student independently reads and summarises three papers from a reading list,
> then presents a brief synthesis to the mentor in the next meeting.*

---

## Lesson Objectives

> *(To be filled in by EROP team.)*

---

## Resources

- [Omniscient Reader's Viewpoint (ORV)](orv.qmd) — tips on reading research papers
- [SSS Class Content Creator (3SC)](sss-content.qmd) — tips on academic writing
